title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: formatting differences — spaces vs underscores, minor spelling variants, taxonomic synonyms — exactly prepR4pcm resolves.
code: data(avonet_subset)    # AVONET morphological traits (Tobias et al. 2022) data(tree_jetz)    # Jetz et al. (2012) phylogeny, Corvoidea + allies  cat(sprintf("Data: %d species\n", nrow(avonet_subset))) #> Data: 919 species cat(sprintf("Tree: %d tips\n", ape::Ntip(tree_jetz))) #> Tree: 657 tips  # The data uses spaces; the tree uses underscores head(avonet_subset$Species1, 3) #> [1] "Acanthiza apicalis"    "Acanthiza chrysorrhoa" "Acanthiza cinerea" head(tree_jetz$tip.label, 3) #> [1] "Amytornis_barbatus"  "Amytornis_merrotsyi" "Amytornis_dorotheae"

what: Step 2: Reconcile data against the tree
title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: reconciliation object records every name-matching decision. Inspect mapping see happened: detailed report:
code: result <- reconcile_tree(   x = avonet_subset,   tree = tree_jetz,   x_species = "Species1",   authority = NULL       # skip synonym lookup for speed ) #> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips #> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages... #> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching... #> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)... #> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips print(result) #>  #> ── Reconciliation: data vs tree ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #>   Source x: avonet_subset #>   Source y: phylo (657 tips) #>   Authority: none #>   Timestamp: 2026-04-06 15:36:26 #> ℹ Match coverage: [█████████████████████░░░░░░░░░] 71% (657/919) #>  #> ── Match summary ── #>  #> • Exact: 0 ( 0.0%) #> • Normalized: 657 (71.5%) #> • Synonym: 0 ( 0.0%) #> • Fuzzy: 0 ( 0.0%) #> • Manual: 0 ( 0.0%) #> ! Unresolved (x only):262 (28.5%) #> ! Unresolved (y only):0 #> ! Flagged for review: 0 #> ℹ Use `reconcile_summary()` for details, `reconcile_mapping()` for the full table. mapping <- reconcile_mapping(result)  # Match type breakdown table(mapping$match_type) #>  #> normalized unresolved  #>        657        262  # Show normalised matches (formatting differences resolved automatically) norm <- mapping[mapping$match_type == "normalized",                 c("name_x", "name_y", "notes")] if (nrow(norm) > 0) head(norm, 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   name_x                name_y                notes                              #>   <chr>                 <chr>                 <chr>                              #> 1 Acanthiza apicalis    Acanthiza_apicalis    'Acanthiza apicalis' normalised t… #> 2 Acanthiza chrysorrhoa Acanthiza_chrysorrhoa 'Acanthiza chrysorrhoa' normalise… #> 3 Acanthiza ewingii     Acanthiza_ewingii     'Acanthiza ewingii' normalised to… #> 4 Acanthiza inornata    Acanthiza_inornata    'Acanthiza inornata' normalised t… #> 5 Acanthiza iredalei    Acanthiza_iredalei    'Acanthiza iredalei' normalised t…  # Unresolved: in data but not in tree unresolved <- mapping[mapping$match_type == "unresolved" & mapping$in_x, ] cat(sprintf("\nSpecies in data but not in tree: %d\n", nrow(unresolved))) #>  #> Species in data but not in tree: 262 reconcile_summary(result, detail = "mismatches_only")

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: Drop unresolved species get matched data frame tree, ready comparative analysis:
code: aligned <- reconcile_apply(   result,   data = avonet_subset,   tree = tree_jetz,   species_col = "Species1",   drop_unresolved = TRUE ) #> ! Dropped 262 rows with unresolved species from data #> ℹ Tree has 657 tips after alignment  cat(sprintf("Aligned data: %d rows\nAligned tree: %d tips\n",             nrow(aligned$data), ape::Ntip(aligned$tree))) #> Aligned data: 657 rows #> Aligned tree: 657 tips

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: PGLS accounts shared evolutionary history estimating regression parameters:
code: library(caper)  # Prepare for caper aligned$data$species <- gsub(" ", "_", aligned$data$Species1) cd <- comparative.data(aligned$tree, aligned$data,                        names.col = "species", vcv = TRUE)  # PGLS: body mass ~ wing length model <- pgls(log(Mass) ~ log(Wing.Length), data = cd) summary(model)

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: non-Gaussian responses (binary, count, proportional data) need random effects beyond phylogeny, use PGLMM. MCMCglmm package fits Bayesian phylogenetic mixed models: See Hadfield (2010, J. Stat. Softw. 33:1–22) MCMCglmm details, Nakagawa & Schielzeth (2013, J. Evol. Biol. 23:494–508) Villemereuil et al. (2025, J. Evol. Biol. 38:1699–1723) guidance phylogenetic mixed models interpretation. complete core workflow: load, reconcile, apply, analyse.
code: library(MCMCglmm)  # Prepare: species as a factor matching tree tip labels aligned$data$phylo <- gsub(" ", "_", aligned$data$Species1)  # Inverse phylogenetic covariance matrix inv_phylo <- inverseA(aligned$tree, nodes = "TIPS", scale = TRUE)  # PGLMM: binary response (e.g., migration status) prior <- list(R = list(V = 1, fix = 1),               G = list(G1 = list(V = 1, nu = 0.002)))  model <- MCMCglmm(   Migration ~ log(Mass) + Trophic.Level,   random = ~phylo,   family = "categorical",   ginverse = list(phylo = inv_phylo$Ainv),   data = aligned$data,   prior = prior,   nitt = 50000, burnin = 10000, thin = 20 ) summary(model)

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: need harmonise species names across two trait datasets matching tree, use reconcile_data(): reconciled, merge two datasets single data frame:
code: data(nesttrait_subset)   # Nest traits (Chia et al. 2023)  rec_data <- reconcile_data(   x = nesttrait_subset,   y = avonet_subset,   x_species = "Scientific_name",   y_species = "Species1",   authority = NULL,   quiet = TRUE ) print(rec_data) #>  #> ── Reconciliation: data vs data ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #>   Source x: nesttrait_subset #>   Source y: avonet_subset #>   Authority: none #>   Timestamp: 2026-04-06 15:36:27 #> ℹ Match coverage: [██████████████████████████████] 100% (916/916) #>  #> ── Match summary ── #>  #> • Exact: 916 (100.0%) #> • Normalized: 0 ( 0.0%) #> • Synonym: 0 ( 0.0%) #> • Fuzzy: 0 ( 0.0%) #> • Manual: 0 ( 0.0%) #> ! Unresolved (x only):0 ( 0.0%) #> ! Unresolved (y only):3 #> ! Flagged for review: 0 #> ℹ Use `reconcile_summary()` for details, `reconcile_mapping()` for the full table. merged <- reconcile_merge(   rec_data,   data_x = nesttrait_subset,   data_y = avonet_subset,   species_col_x = "Scientific_name",   species_col_y = "Species1" ) #> ✔ Merged 916 species (inner join) cat(sprintf("Merged: %d rows, %d columns\n", nrow(merged), ncol(merged))) #> Merged: 916 rows, 31 columns

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: data tree use different taxonomies (e.g., BirdLife data BirdTree phylogeny), curated crosswalk can resolve names automated synonym resolution misses. crosswalk simply table mapping names one system another. prepR4pcm includes BirdLife-BirdTree crosswalk example: Convert overrides table pass reconcile_tree(): need crosswalk? data tree follow different naming authorities curated mapping exists. use cases, automatic cascade (exact → normalised → synonym) sufficient. can also build overrides manually — just data frame name_x, name_y, optionally user_note columns:
code: data(crosswalk_birdlife_birdtree) table(crosswalk_birdlife_birdtree$Match.type) #>  #>              1BL to 1BT          1BL to many BT                 Extinct  #>                    8960                     225                     143  #>          Many BL to 1BT Newly described species  #>                    1933                      24 overrides <- reconcile_crosswalk(   crosswalk_birdlife_birdtree,   from_col = "Species1",   to_col = "Species3",   match_type_col = "Match.type" ) #> ℹ 1933 many-to-one entries (lumps) included #> ℹ 225 one-to-many entries (splits) included #> ✔ Crosswalk: 3039 overrides (8079 identical pairs skipped)  # Re-reconcile with overrides result_xw <- reconcile_tree(   x = avonet_subset,   tree = tree_jetz,   x_species = "Species1",   authority = NULL,   overrides = overrides ) #> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips #> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages... #> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching... #> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (68 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)... #> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips  # Compare: how many more matches with the crosswalk? cat(sprintf("Without crosswalk: %d matched\n",             sum(result$mapping$in_x & result$mapping$in_y, na.rm = TRUE))) #> Without crosswalk: 657 matched cat(sprintf("With crosswalk:    %d matched\n",             sum(result_xw$mapping$in_x & result_xw$mapping$in_y, na.rm = TRUE))) #> With crosswalk:    657 matched my_overrides <- data.frame(   name_x    = c("Old name A", "Old name B"),   name_y    = c("Tree name A", "Tree name B"),   user_note = c("Reclassified in 2023", "Spelling correction") ) result <- reconcile_tree(my_data, my_tree, overrides = my_overrides)

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: sensitivity analyses across phylogenies, reconcile_to_trees() reconciles one dataset several trees one call:
code: data(tree_clements25)  # Clements 2025 tree  results <- reconcile_to_trees(   x = avonet_subset,   trees = list(     jetz      = tree_jetz,     clements  = tree_clements25   ),   x_species = "Species1",   authority = NULL ) #> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names against 2 trees #> ℹ   [jetz] 657 tips #> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages... #> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching... #> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)... #> ✔   [jetz] Matched 657/919 names #> ℹ   [clements] 854 tips #> ℹ Matching 919 x 854 names through 2 stages... #> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching... #> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (854 matched so far)... #> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (854 matched so far)... #> ✔   [clements] Matched 854/919 names  # Compare overlap across trees sapply(results, function(r) {   c(matched = sum(r$mapping$in_x & r$mapping$in_y, na.rm = TRUE),     unresolved_x = r$counts$n_unresolved_x) }) #>              jetz clements #> matched       657      854 #> unresolved_x  262       65

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: Enable fuzzy matching catch likely typos species names:
code: result <- reconcile_tree(   x = my_data,   tree = my_tree,   fuzzy = TRUE,              # enable fuzzy matching   fuzzy_threshold = 0.9,     # minimum similarity (0-1)   resolve = "flag"           # flag low-confidence matches for review )  # Check flagged matches flagged <- reconcile_mapping(result) flagged[flagged$match_type == "flagged", c("name_x", "name_y", "match_score")]

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: tree fewer species data, reconcile_apply() drops unresolved species. loses statistical power can bias sample. reconcile_augment() grafts missing species onto tree using genus-level placement: Use augmented tree downstream analyses: Important caveat. Genus-level placement assumes missing species diverged similarly congeners, may hold. Always report species augmented run sensitivity analyses comparing results without .
code:
aug <- reconcile_augment(   result,   tree_jetz,   where = "genus",                # sister to a random congener    branch_length = "congener_median",  # median terminal branch of congeners   seed = 42,                      # for reproducibility   quiet = TRUE )  cat(sprintf("Original tips: %d
Augmented tips: %d
",             ape::Ntip(aug$original), ape::Ntip(aug$tree))) #> Original tips: 657 #> Augmented tips: 793 cat(sprintf("Added: %d | Skipped (no congener): %d
",             nrow(aug$augmented), nrow(aug$skipped))) #> Added: 136 | Skipped (no congener): 126  # Which species were added, and where? if (nrow(aug$augmented) > 0) head(aug$augmented[, c("species", "placed_near", "branch_length")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   species                  placed_near             branch_length #>   <chr>                    <chr>                           <dbl> #> 1 Acanthiza cinerea        Acanthiza lineata                5.04 #> 2 Calamanthus cautus       Calamanthus fuliginosus          3.80 #> 3 Calamanthus montanellus  Calamanthus fuliginosus          3.80 #> 4 Calamanthus pyrrhopygius Calamanthus fuliginosus          3.80 #> 5 Gerygone citrina         Gerygone magnirostris            2.91 #> 6 Pyrrholaemus sagittatus  Pyrrholaemus brunneus           15.5 aligned_aug <- reconcile_apply(   result,   data = avonet_subset,   tree = aug$tree,   species_col = "Species1",   drop_unresolved = TRUE )

title: From Raw Data to PGLS: A Complete Bird Trait Workflow
text: Taxon-agnostic. workflow works group — mammals, fish, amphibians, plants — long data frame phylogenetic tree. Provenance. Every name-matching decision recorded reconciliation object. Use reconcile_summary() human-readable report reconcile_mapping() full table. Crosswalks optional. users need . automatic cascade handles formatting differences synonyms. Crosswalks help two well-known naming authorities disagree. Tree augmentation. tree incomplete, reconcile_augment() grafts missing species using congener placement — always run sensitivity analyses without augmented tips. Sensitivity. reconcile_to_trees() makes easy run analysis across multiple phylogenies. Merging. reconcile_merge() joins two reconciled datasets single analysis-ready data frame, using mapping join key. Reports. reconcile_report() generates self-contained HTML report suitable sharing archiving. Visualisation. reconcile_plot() produces bar pie chart match composition. reconcile_suggest() shows closest fuzzy candidates unresolved species. Comparison. reconcile_diff() compares two reconciliation runs side side — e.g., adding crosswalk.

title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
text: Mismatches data tree arise : - Formatting: Homo_sapiens vs Homo sapiens, trailing whitespace, attached authority strings - Synonymy: Taxonomic revisions move species genera - Missing names: Species data tree (vice versa) Fixing hand tedious, error-prone, poorly documented. prepR4pcm solves structured matching cascade: exact match → normalised match → synonym resolution. Every decision recorded.

title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("itchyshin/prepR4pcm") library(prepR4pcm)

what: Example 1: Reconcile a dataset against a tree
title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
text: Suppose trait data phylogenetic tree slightly different naming conventions. Notice mismatches: - Pan_troglodytes data underscore; tree uses underscores , data column uses mix - Cebus capucinus data tree - Papio anubis tree data
code: # Simulated trait data trait_data <- data.frame(   species = c(     "Homo sapiens",     "Pan_troglodytes",       # underscore instead of space     "Gorilla gorilla",     "Pongo pygmaeus",     "Macaca mulatta",     "Cebus capucinus"   ),   body_mass = c(70, 50, 160, 80, 8, 3),   brain_mass = c(1.35, 0.39, 0.50, 0.37, 0.11, 0.07) )  # Simulated phylogenetic tree (built manually for this example) tree <- ape::read.tree(text = paste0(   "((((Homo_sapiens:5,Pan_troglodytes:5):3,",   "Gorilla_gorilla:8):4,Pongo_pygmaeus:12):6,",   "(Macaca_mulatta:10,Papio_anubis:10):8);" )) result <- reconcile_tree(   x = trait_data,   tree = tree,   x_species = "species",   authority = NULL,        # skip synonym lookup for this example   quiet = FALSE ) #> ℹ Reconciling 6 data names vs 6 tree tips #> ✔ Matched 5/6 data names to tree tips

title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
text: names differ due synonymy, pass authority enable synonym resolution via taxadb: Supported authorities: col, itis, gbif, ncbi, ott, fb, slb, wd, iucn, itis_test. database downloaded cached locally, ensuring reproducibility — results depend external API available.
code: # Requires taxadb and a local database download (automatic on first use) result3 <- reconcile_tree(   x = trait_data,   tree = tree,   x_species = "species",   authority = "col"        # Catalogue of Life )

title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
text: Labs often maintain curated list known corrections. can pass data frame CSV path: Overrides applied matching stage, always take priority.
code: # A data frame of known corrections corrections <- data.frame(   name_x = c("Corvus sp.", "Turdus merulaa"),   name_y = c("Corvus corax", "Turdus merula"),   user_note = c("Only one Corvus in our tree", "Typo in source data") )  result4 <- reconcile_tree(   x = my_data,   tree = my_tree,   overrides = corrections )  # Or from a CSV file: result5 <- reconcile_tree(   x = my_data,   tree = my_tree,   overrides = "lab_corrections.csv" )

title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
text: Conservative: Names never silently changed. Ambiguous cases flagged, auto-resolved. Transparent: Every decision recorded match type, score, source, human-readable note. Reproducible: Database versions pinned. parameters stored reconciliation object. Practical: Works objects comparative biologists already use — data.frame ape::phylo.

title: Getting Started with prepR4pcm
code: library(prepR4pcm)  # 1. Load your data and tree my_data <- read.csv("species_traits.csv") my_tree <- ape::read.tree("species_tree.nwk")  # 2. Reconcile result <- reconcile_tree(my_data, my_tree, authority = "col")  # 3. Review print(result) reconcile_summary(result, detail = "mismatches_only")  # 4. Fix manually if needed result <- reconcile_override(result, "Corvus sp.", "Corvus corax",                              note = "Only one Corvus in tree")  # 5. Apply aligned <- reconcile_apply(result, data = my_data, tree = my_tree,                             drop_unresolved = TRUE)  # 6. Analyse # aligned$data and aligned$tree are ready for caper, phytools, MCMCglmm, etc.

title: Prepare Data and Trees for Phylogenetic Comparative Methods
text: prepR4pcm reconciles species names across datasets phylogenetic trees comparative biology workflows. Taxonomic mismatches — formatting differences, synonymy, typos — persistent source silent data loss phylogenetic comparative methods (PCM). package detects resolves mismatches multi-stage matching cascade, producing documented, reproducible alignments data trees.

title: Prepare Data and Trees for Phylogenetic Comparative Methods
text: Core matching — four-stage cascade resolves names progressively: Exact — identical strings Normalised — case, whitespace, underscore differences Synonym — taxonomic synonym lookup via local databases (optional) Fuzzy — Levenshtein-based similarity genus pre-filtering (~100x faster naive pairwise comparison) Diagnostics — every decision recorded mapping table. Functions like reconcile_summary(), reconcile_plot(), reconcile_report(), reconcile_suggest() make easy audit matches, visualise coverage, find near-misses unresolved species. Multi-tree support — reconcile_to_trees() matches dataset several trees , reconcile_diff() compares results across trees parameter settings. Crosswalks overrides — reconcile_crosswalk() converts published taxonomy crosswalks (e.g., BirdLife–BirdTree) override tables. reconcile_override() reconcile_override_batch() let inject manual corrections stage. Advanced — reconcile_augment() grafts unresolved species onto tree using genus-level placement. reconcile_splits_lumps() detects taxonomic splits lumps synonym resolution results.

title: prepR4pcm: Prepare Data and Trees for Phylogenetic Comparative Methods — prepR4pcm-package
text: Reconcile species names across datasets phylogenetic trees comparative biology workflows. Identifies mismatches due formatting differences, taxonomic synonymy, spelling errors. Produces detailed reports documenting name resolved, taxonomic authority used, remains unresolved. Supports exact matching, name normalisation, synonym resolution via local taxonomic databases, fuzzy matching likely typos. Detects taxonomic splits lumps.

code: reconcile_data(   x,   y,   x_species = NULL,   y_species = NULL,   authority = "col",   rank = c("species", "subspecies"),   overrides = NULL,   db_version = NULL,   fuzzy = FALSE,   fuzzy_threshold = 0.9,   resolve = c("flag", "first"),   quiet = FALSE )

title: Reconcile species names between two datasets — reconcile_data
text: x data frame (first dataset). y data frame (second dataset). x_species Character(1). Column name x containing species names. Auto-detected NULL. y_species Character(1). Column name y containing species names. Auto-detected NULL. authority Character(1). Taxonomic authority synonym resolution: "col" (default), "itis", "gbif", "ncbi", etc. Set NULL skip synonym lookup entirely. rank Character(1). "species" (default) strips infraspecific epithets normalisation. "subspecies" retains trinomials. overrides data frame least columns name_x name_y containing pre-built name corrections, file path CSV. Optional column user_note documentation. Set NULL (default) overrides. db_version Character(1). taxadb database version. NULL uses latest available. fuzzy Logical. Enable fuzzy matching likely typos? Default FALSE. TRUE, names remain unmatched synonym resolution compared using component-based string similarity. fuzzy_threshold Numeric (0–1). Minimum similarity score fuzzy matches. Default 0.9 (conservative; catches obvious typos). resolve Character(1). handle low-confidence matches: "flag" (default) marks fuzzy matches 0.95 indirect synonym matches match_type = "flagged" manual review. "first" accepts matches face value. quiet Logical. Suppress progress messages? Default FALSE.

title: prepR4pcm 0.3.0
text: reconcile_report() generates self-contained HTML report documenting name-matching decisions — suitable sharing archiving. reconcile_merge() joins two reconciled datasets single analysis-ready data frame using mapping table reconcile_data(). reconcile_augment() grafts unresolved species onto tree using genus-level placement (sister congener MRCA congeners). reconcile_splits_lumps() detects taxonomic splits lumps synonym resolution results. Fuzzy matching via fuzzy = TRUE catches likely typos using component-based Levenshtein similarity. resolve = "flag" marks low-confidence matches manual review. reconcile_plot() visualises match composition bar chart pie chart using base R graphics. reconcile_suggest() shows closest fuzzy candidates unresolved species — useful finding near-misses. reconcile_diff() compares two reconciliation objects reports gained/lost matches, type changes, target changes. reconcile_override_batch() applies multiple overrides data frame CSV file. reconcile_review() provides interactive console interface accepting rejecting flagged fuzzy matches one time. print.reconciliation() now shows coverage bar: [████████████████████░░░░░░░░░░] 71% (657/919). Stage-level progress messages large datasets (> 500 species) matching cascade.

title: prepR4pcm 0.3.0
text: Example datasets expanded ~200 ~920 species (Corvoidea + allied passerine families) realistic demonstrations. \dontrun{} examples replaced runnable examples using bundled data.

title: prepR4pcm 0.3.0
text: Fuzzy matching (pr_fuzzy_match()) now uses genus pre-filtering: species whose genus within 2 edits compared. reduces computation O(n×m) roughly O(n×k) k number congeners+near-genera, giving ~100× speedup large datasets (e.g., 1360×6504 >10 min ~3 sec). reconcile_suggest() uses genus pre-filter vectorised adist(), making usable 1000+ unresolved species.

title: prepR4pcm 0.3.0
text: Fixed crosswalk overrides effect. cascade’s override pre-stage required exact string matches override names input names. override names used spaces tree tips used underscores (vice versa), overrides applied. Overrides now normalise sides comparison. Fixed reconcile_plot() error passing main argument: internal pr_plot_bar() hardcoded main also passed ..., causing duplicate argument error. Fixed .Rbuildignore patterns excluded .rda data files case-insensitive filesystems.

title: prepR4pcm 0.2.0
text: Core reconciliation engine: exact → normalised → synonym → fuzzy cascade. reconcile_tree(), reconcile_data(), reconcile_trees(). reconcile_apply(), reconcile_export(), reconcile_override(). reconcile_to_trees(), reconcile_multi(), reconcile_crosswalk(). reconcile_summary(), reconcile_mapping(). Bundled datasets: avonet_subset, nesttrait_subset, delhey_subset, crosswalk_birdlife_birdtree, tree_jetz, tree_clements25. Bird workflow vignette.
